rule_metadata.author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
rule_metadata.title: Obfuscated Commands - Command Shell

rules.rule_text:
title: Obfuscated Commands - Command Shell
id: b58b6c4b-0f79-407e-b2ab-76de16a6cd79
status: experimental
description: "Detecting obfuscation in the command shell is relatively straightforward,\
  \ but \nthere are a lot of variations to consider when you\u2019re developing detection\
  \ \ncoverage. Of course, the process you\u2019re looking for will always be cmd.exe,\
  \ \nbut the corresponding command line can contain a variety of suspicious characters.\
  \ \nThe trick is finding the thresholds where the inclusion of obfuscation characters\
  \ \ngo from normal to anomalous (but benign) to suspicious enough to warrant alerting.\
  \ \nThe following pseudo-detection logic offers a good starting point for developing\
  \ \ndetection coverage for obfuscation in the command line. Part of the RedCanary\
  \ 2024 \nThreat Detection Report.\n"
references:
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/techniques/windows-command-shell/
author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
date: 2024/03/21
tags:
- attack.execution
- attack.t1059
- attack.t1059.003
- attack.defense_evasion
- attack.t1027
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: \cmd.exe
    CommandLine|re: ^([^^=%![(; ]*[\^=%![(; ]){8,}[^^=%![(; ]*$
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
- Unknown
level: low
